question_text: Which of the following statements correctly describes the difference between evaporation and boiling?

options.label: A
options.text: Evaporation only happens at 100°C, while boiling can happen at any temperature

options.label: B
options.text: Boiling happens at a specific temperature throughout the liquid, while evaporation happens at the surface at any temperature

options.label: C
options.text: Evaporation produces steam while boiling produces water vapour

options.label: D
options.text: Boiling is faster than evaporation because it uses more heat energy

explanation: Boiling occurs at a fixed temperature (100°C for water) and happens throughout the liquid with vigorous bubbling. Evaporation happens at the surface at any temperature when particles have enough energy to escape.
